F.description: Proportion of cat paw treats in the initial blend

S.description: Profit earned per each kilogram of the initial blend

R.description: Quantity of cat paw treats in stock

Z.description: Revenue generated for each kilogram of the alternate blend

W.description: Gold shark treats in stock by weight.

M.description: The proportion of feline paw treats in the second blend

assumptions.description: The proportion of cat paw treats in the initial blend must be non-negative.
The profit earned per each kilogram of the initial blend must be non-negative.
The quantity of cat paw treats in stock must be non-negative.
The revenue generated for each kilogram of the alternate blend must be non-negative.
The gold shark treats in stock by weight must be non-negative.
The proportion of feline paw treats in the second blend must be non-negative.

v.description: The amount of the second blend in kilograms

n.description: The amount of the initial blend in kilograms

constraints.description: The combined weight of cat paw treats in both blends should not surpass R kilograms, determined by adding together F multiplied by n and M multiplied by v.
The combined weight of gold shark snacks in the two mixes should not go over W kilograms, which is determined as the sum of ((100 - F) multiplied by n) and ((100 - M) multiplied by v).
The amount of the initial blend produced must be non-negative.
The amount of the second blend produced must be non-negative.

objective.description: The objective has been replaced by the solution value.

notes: Random formulation unrelated to the original problem, with the same optimal objective value as the original problem.
